title: ipomoea pes-caprae
intro: Ipomoea pes-caprae, also known as beach morning glory or sea morning glory, is a perennial vine native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world, known for its bright blue or purple flowers and edible seeds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a potential treatment for wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Ipomoea pes-caprae include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be consumed as a powder or capsule, but it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.
